facts.text: Athens hosted the first modern Olympic Games in 1896 and again in 2004, making it one of only five cities to do so twice.
Athens' name traces back to the goddess Athena, though scholars now think the goddess adopted the name from the city.
The Acropolis Museum, a landmark modern addition, sits beside the ancient Acropolis and connects the city’s historic core with a pedestrian walkway.
Multinational tech giants such as Ericsson, Sony, and Microsoft maintain regional research and development headquarters in Athens, reflecting its growing tech scene.
